missing_spf.summary: Add an SPF (Sender Policy Framework) DNS record to authorize which servers can send email from your domain
steps: Log into your DNS provider (Cloudflare, GoDaddy, Namecheap, Route53, etc.)
Navigate to DNS settings for your domain
Add a new TXT record
Name/Host: @ (or your domain name)
Value: v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all (adjust for your email provider)
For Microsoft 365: v=spf1 include:spf.protection.outlook.com -all
For custom: v=spf1 ip4:YOUR_SERVER_IP -all
TTL: 3600 (1 hour) or default
Save the record and wait 24-48 hours for propagation
Verify: nslookup -type=txt yourdomain.com or use our re-scan tool

missing_dkim.summary: Generate and add a DKIM key to authenticate your outgoing email
steps: Log into your email provider's admin console
Navigate to security / authentication settings
Generate a new DKIM key (usually 2048-bit recommended)
Copy the generated DNS record
Go to your DNS provider and add the TXT record
Name/Host: default._domainkey (or as provided)
Paste the DKIM value
Enable DKIM signing in your email provider settings
Verify with our re-scan tool

missing_dmarc.summary: Add a DMARC policy to control how receivers handle unauthenticated email
steps: Go to your DNS provider's settings
Add a new TXT record
Name/Host: _dmarc
Value: v=DMARC1; p=reject; rua=mailto:security@yourdomain.com; pct=100
Start with p=none (monitoring) → p=quarantine → p=reject over 2-4 weeks
Set rua to receive daily XML reports on email authentication
TTL: 3600 or default
Save and wait for propagation

no_hsts.summary: Add HTTP Strict-Transport-Security header to force HTTPS connections
steps: Edit your web server configuration (Nginx, Apache, etc.)
Add the HSTS header with a 1-year max-age
Test with a short max-age first (e.g., 300 seconds)
Once confirmed, increase to 31536000 (1 year)
Include preload directive if submitting to browser preload lists

code_snippets.label: Apache .htaccess
code_snippets.code: Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload"

code_snippets.label: Cloudflare
code_snippets.code: Enable "Always Use HTTPS" and "Automatic HTTPS Rewrites" in SSL/TLS > Edge Certificates

no_csp.summary: Implement Content Security Policy to prevent XSS and data injection attacks
steps: Start with report-only mode: Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only
Check your site for inline scripts and external resources
Build a CSP policy that whitelists your trusted sources
Switch to enforcement: Content-Security-Policy header
Monitor reports and adjust as needed

code_snippets.label: Nginx - Basic CSP
code_snippets.code: add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none';" always;

weak_ssl.summary: Upgrade SSL/TLS configuration to use modern, secure ciphers
steps: Check your current SSL config with SSL Labs or testssl.sh
Disable TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 (keep TLS 1.2 and 1.3)
Disable weak ciphers (RC4, 3DES, CBC-mode ciphers)
Enable only AEAD ciphers (AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305)
Restart your web server and re-test

code_snippets.label: Nginx - Modern SSL Config

missing_x_frame_options.summary: Add X-Frame-Options header to prevent clickjacking
steps: Add the header to prevent your site from being embedded in iframes
Use DENY if your site should never be framed
Use SAMEORIGIN if you need to frame your own content
Keep all software and systems up to date
Enable logging and monitoring for suspicious activity
Implement principle of least privilege for all accounts
Use multi-factor authentication everywhere
Conduct regular security audits and penetration tests
Have an incident response plan ready
